task_completion.description: Create the target menu engineering analysis workbook.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A spreadsheet application (e.g., LibreOffice Calc) is open with the filename 'menu_engineering_analysis.xlsx' visible in the title bar or save dialog.

task_completion.description: Calculate per-item food cost percentages, contribution margins, and total quantities sold.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Spreadsheet columns clearly labeled for Food Cost % (or FC%), Contribution Margin (or CM), and Quantity Sold (or Qty), populated with numerical data for the menu items.

task_completion.description: Classify every item using the Kasavana-Smith Menu Engineering Matrix.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A column in the spreadsheet containing the specific classification terms: 'Star', 'Plowhorse', 'Puzzle', and 'Dog' assigned to the various menu items.

task_completion.description: Include the overall weighted food cost percentage.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A specific cell or summary section displaying the 'Overall Food Cost' or 'Weighted Food Cost %', showing a value between 30.0% and 40.0% (expected to be ~30.3%).

task_completion.description: Organize the workbook with a professional multi-sheet structure featuring summary statistics and category breakdowns.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Multiple sheet tabs visible at the bottom of the spreadsheet interface (e.g., 'Data', 'Summary', 'Categories') AND a sheet displaying aggregated summary statistics or category breakdowns.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used a proper spreadsheet application to structure and format the .xlsx file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent interacting with a spreadsheet GUI (like LibreOffice Calc) to view, format, or create the multi-sheet workbook, rather than just writing a raw text/CSV file and renaming the extension.

integrity.description: Verify the data appears to be genuinely processed from the source files.
integrity.visual_evidence: The spreadsheet contains exactly 30 menu items with calculated decimals, and the formula bar (if visible) shows actual spreadsheet formulas (e.g., =B2/C2) rather than hardcoded plain text answers.

integrity.description: Verify no hallucinated classification categories were used.
integrity.visual_evidence: The classification column ONLY contains the terms 'Star', 'Plowhorse', 'Puzzle', or 'Dog'. No other terms (like 'Loser', 'Winner', 'Average') are present.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected output path: /home/ga/Documents/Spreadsheets/menu_engineering_analysis.xlsx. The dataset contains exactly 30 menu items. The required matrix classifications are exactly 'star', 'plowhorse', 'puzzle', and 'dog'. The overall weighted food cost percentage should be between 30.0% and 40.0% (specifically around 30.30%).
